blocks:
Surah Al-Mulk

It is also called Surah Al-Munjiya (The Deliverer) because it delivers its reciter from the punishment of the grave, and it is called Al-Waqiya (The Protector). It is Makkan, and the number of its verses is thirty-one. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١)تَبارَكَ الَّذِي بِيَدِهِ الْمُلْكُ (1) Blessed is He in Whose hand is the sovereignty, [Meaning:] By the grasp of His power is the disposal of all affairs, وَهُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ. (and He has power over all things.)

(٢)الَّذِي خَلَقَ الْمَوْتَ وَالْحَياةَ (2) He who created death and life, Al-Qummi said: He decreed them both, and its meaning is: He decreed life, then death. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah created life before death. And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Life and death are two creations among the creations of Allah. When death comes and enters a human being, it does not enter anything except that life has already departed from it. لِيَبْلُوَكُمْ (that He may test you) [Meaning:] that He may treat you with the treatment of the examiner through obligation (التكليف), أَيُّكُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلاً (as to which of you is best in conduct.) And that is because death is a caller to good conduct and necessitates the lack of reliance upon the world (الدنيا) and its fleeting pleasures, while life provides the ability to perform righteous, sincere deeds. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He was asked about His saying: أَيُّكُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلاً (as to which of you is best in conduct), what was meant by it? He replied: He means: which of you is best in intellect (عقلاً). Then He said: The most complete of you in intellect, the most intense of you in fear of Allah, and the best of you in considering what Allah has commanded and forbidden, even if they are the least of you in voluntary acts (تطوّعاً). And in another narration: Which of you is best in intellect (عقلاً), the most abstinent concerning the prohibitions of Allah (محارم الله), and the quickest in obedience to Allah. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He does not mean the most in deed (عملاً), but rather the most correct of you in deed (عملاً). And correctness (الإصابة) is only through the fear of Allah (خشية الله) and sincere intention (النيّة الصادقة). Then He said: Maintaining the deed (الابقاء على العمل) until it becomes sincere (يخلص) is harder than the deed itself. And the sincere deed (العمل الخالص) is that for which you do not desire anyone to praise you except Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. And the intention (النيّة) is better than the deed. Beware, the intention (النيّة) is the deed itself. Then He recited the saying of the Mighty and Majestic: قُلْ كُلٌّ يَعْمَلُ عَلى شاكِلَتِهِ (Say: ‘Everyone acts according to his own disposition.’) meaning: his intention (نيّته). I say: Perhaps what is meant by maintaining the deed (الابقاء على العمل) is that one does not speak of it desiring praise from people, so that it remains sincere (خالصاً) for Allah. And it is not hidden that this is harder than the deed itself. وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ (and He is the All-mighty) [Meaning:] The Dominant One (الغالب) Whom the one who performs evil deeds cannot frustrate. الْغَفُورُ (the All-forgiving.) [Meaning:] For those among them who repent.

(٣) الَّذِي خَلَقَ سَبْعَ سَماواتٍ طِباقاً ** (3) Who created seven heavens in layers. conformity (مطابقة). Al-Qummi narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Some of them are above others. ** ما تَرى فِي خَلْقِ الرَّحْمنِ مِنْ تَفاوُتٍ ** You do not see any disparity in the creation of the All-beneficent. [meaning] discrepancy (اختلاف). Al-Qummi said: meaning corruption (فساد). And it was read (تفوّت), and it is with the same meaning. ** فَارْجِعِ الْبَصَرَ هَلْ تَرى مِنْ فُطُورٍ ** Look again: do you see any flaw? [meaning] defect (خلل). He said: meaning, you have looked at them repeatedly, so look at them one more time, contemplating them, so that you may witness what I have informed you of regarding their proportion (تناسبها) and uprightness (استقامتها).

(٤) ثُمَّ ارْجِعِ الْبَصَرَ كَرَّتَيْنِ ** (4) Then look again meaning two other returns (رجعتين اخريين) in seeking out the defect (الخلل). And what is intended by the dual form (التثنية) is repetition (التكرير) and multiplication (التكثير), just as in لَبَّيْكَ and سَعْدَيْكَ. And al-Qummi said: Look into the dominion (ملكوت) of the heavens and the earth. يَنْقَلِبْ إِلَيْكَ الْبَصَرُ خاسِئاً ** the sight will return to you humbled far from attaining what is sought (اصابة المطلوب), as if it were driven away from it with humiliation (بالصّغار). وَهُوَ حَسِيرٌ ** while it is weary. exhausted (كليل) from the length of the repetition (طول المعاودة) and the abundance of review (كثرة المراجعة).

(٥)** وَلَقَدْ زَيَّنَّا السَّماءَ الدُّنْيا بِمَصابِيحَ وَجَعَلْناها رُجُوماً لِلشَّياطِينِ وَأَعْتَدْنا لَهُمْ عَذابَ السَّعِيرِ ** (5) Certainly We have adorned the lowest heaven with lamps, and We have made them missiles for [pelting] the devils, and We have prepared for them the punishment of the Blaze.

The Almighty's saying: ** وَلَقَدْ زَيَّنَّا السَّماءَ الدُّنْيا ** which is the heaven closest to the earth ** بِمَصابِيحَ ** Al-Qummi said: with the stars (بالنجوم). ** وَجَعَلْناها رُجُوماً لِلشَّياطِينِ ** Rujum (رُجُوماً) is the plural of rajm (رَجْم), with a fatḥah [on the rāʾ], meaning that with which one pelts. It was said that what is meant by it is the falling of the meteors (الشّهب) caused by them. And it was said: meaning missiles (رُجُوماً) and conjectures (ظُنُوناً) for the devils among mankind, who are the astrologers (المنجمون). ** وَأَعْتَدْنا لَهُمْ عَذابَ السَّعِيرِ ** in the Hereafter, after being burned by the meteors (بالشّهب) in this world (الدنيا).

(6) وَلِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِرَبِّهِمْ من الشياطين وغيرهم عَذابُ جَهَنَّمَ وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ. (6) As for those who defy their Lord, there is the punishment of hell, and what a wretched destiny! — from the devils (الشياطين) and others —

(7) إِذا أُلْقُوا فِيها سَمِعُوا لَها شَهِيقاً صوتاً كصوت الحمير وَهِيَ تَفُورُ تغلي بهم غليان المرجل بما فيه. (7) When they are cast into it, they will hear it sighing while it boils. — a sound (صوتاً) like the braying of donkeys (كصوت الحمير) — وَهِيَ تَفُورُ ** (while it boils) — it boils with them, like the boiling of a cauldron (المرجل) with what is inside it.

(8) تَكادُ تَمَيَّزُ مِنَ الْغَيْظِ تتفرّق غضباً عليهم وهو تمثيل لشدّة اشتعالها. (8) It is almost bursting with rage. — it splits apart (تتفرّق) out of anger (غضباً) towards them, and this is a metaphor (تمثيل) for the intensity of its blazing. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: [It is bursting] out of rage (الغيظ) against the enemies of Allah. كُلَّما أُلْقِيَ فِيها فَوْجٌ ** (Whenever a group is cast into it) — a multitude (جماعة) of them — سَأَلَهُمْ خَزَنَتُها أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ نَذِيرٌ ** (its keepers will ask them, 'Did no warner come to you?') — who warns you of this punishment (العذاب), and this is a rebuke (توبيخ) and condemnation (تبكيت).

(٩) ** قالُوا بَلى قَدْ جاءَنا نَذِيرٌ فَكَذَّبْنا وَقُلْنا ما نَزَّلَ اللهُ مِنْ شَيْءٍ إِنْ أَنْتُمْ إِلَّا فِي ضَلالٍ كَبِيرٍ ** (9) They will say, 'Yes, a warner certainly came to us, but we denied and said, "Allah has not sent down anything; you are only in a great error." ' That is, so we denied the Messengers and we went to excess in denial, until we negated the sending down (الإنزال) and the sending (الإرسال) entirely, and we went to extremes in attributing error (الضلال) to them.

(١٠) ** وَقالُوا لَوْ كُنَّا نَسْمَعُ ** (10) And they will say: 'Had we listened (to) the speech (كلام) of the Messengers, and accepted it wholly (جملة) without investigation (بحث) or scrutiny (تفتيش), relying upon their truthfulness (صدقهم), أَوْ نَعْقِلُ ** or applied reason, and reflected upon its wisdom (حكمه) and its meanings (معانيه), with the reflection (تفكّر) of those who seek insight (المستبصرين), ما كُنَّا فِي أَصْحابِ السَّعِيرِ ** we would not have been among the inmates of the Blaze,' among their number (عدادهم) and within their company (جملتهم).

(١١) فَاعْتَرَفُوا بِذَنْبِهِمْ when it does not benefit them ** فَسُحْقاً لِأَصْحابِ السَّعِيرِ ** (11) So they confessed their sin. So away with the inmates of the Blaze!

So Allah removed them far away, a removal, meaning He distanced them a great distance from His mercy. And it was recited فُسُحُقاً with two ḍammahs (بضمّتين). And Al-Qummi said: They had heard and understood, but they did not obey and did not accept, as their confession of their sin indicates.

In Al-Ihtijaj, in the Prophetic Khutbah of Ghadir: that these verses concern the enemies of Ali and his children (peace be upon them), and the verses that follow concern their allies.

(١٢) ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُمْ بِالْغَيْبِ لَهُمْ مَغْفِرَةٌ ** (12) Indeed those who fear their Lord in secret will have forgiveness for their sins ** وَأَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ ** and a great reward. beside which the pleasures of the world become insignificant. (١٣) ** وَأَسِرُّوا قَوْلَكُمْ أَوِ اجْهَرُوا بِهِ إِنَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ ** (13) Whether you keep your talk secret or disclose it, He indeed knows well what is in the breasts. He knows the inner thoughts (الضمائر) before they are expressed secretly or openly. (١٤) ** أَلا يَعْلَمُ مَنْ خَلَقَ وَهُوَ اللَّطِيفُ الْخَبِيرُ ** (14) Should He who created not know? And He is the All-subtle, the All-aware. Whose knowledge reaches what is manifest of His creation and what is hidden, even if it is small and subtle. Nothing escapes Him, nor does anything elude Him. It is narrated: The polytheists (المشركين) used to speak among themselves about certain things, and Allah would inform His Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم about them. So they would say: ** أَسِرُّوا قَوْلَكُمْ ** (Keep your talk secret) lest the God of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم hear it. So Allah drew attention to their ignorance.

(15) هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَرْضَ ذَلُولاً (15) It is He who made the earth tractable for you. soft (ليّنة), making movement upon it easy for you. فَامْشُوا فِي مَناكِبِها So walk on its shoulders upon its sides (جوانبها) or its mountains (جبالها). It is said that this is a metaphor for extreme subservience (فرط التذلّل), for the shoulder (منكب) of the camel resists the rider stepping upon it and does not become subservient to him. Thus, when the earth is made so subservient (الذلّ) that one walks upon its shoulders (مناكبها), nothing remains of it that has not been made subservient. وَكُلُوا مِنْ رِزْقِهِ and eat of His provision. And seek the bounties (نعم) of Allah. وَإِلَيْهِ النُّشُورُ And toward Him is the resurrection. The return (المرجع), and He will question you concerning what He bestowed upon you.

(١٦) أَأَمِنْتُمْ مَنْ فِي السَّماءِ ** (16) Do you feel secure from Him who is in the heaven meaning the angels entrusted with the administration (تدبير) of this world. And it was recited (وقرئ) as وأمنتم by changing the first hamzah (الهمزة الأولى) into a waw (واوا) due to the preceding letter having a dammah (لانضمام ما قبلها), and by changing the second [hamzah] (الثانية) into an alif (ألفاً). أَنْ يَخْسِفَ بِكُمُ الْأَرْضَ ** that He will make the earth swallow you so He makes you disappear within it, just as He did with Qarun. فَإِذا هِيَ تَمُورُ then behold, it trembles it shakes/is agitated (تضطرب).

** أَمْ أَمِنْتُمْ مَنْ فِي السَّماءِ أَنْ يُرْسِلَ عَلَيْكُمْ حاصِباً ** (Do you feel secure from Him who is in the heaven, that He will not unleash upon you a rain of stones?) ** أَنْ يُرْسِلَ عَلَيْكُمْ حاصِباً ** (that He will not unleash upon you a rain of stones) means that He will rain down stones (حَصَباً) upon you. ** فَسَتَعْلَمُونَ كَيْفَ نَذِيرِ ** (then soon you will know how My warning is) means: how My warning (إِنْذَارِي) is, when you witness that which was warned against (الْمُنْذَر بِهِ). However, the knowledge (الْعِلْم) will not benefit you at that time (حِينَئِذٍ).

** وَلَقَدْ كَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ فَكَيْفَ كانَ نَكِيرِ ** (Certainly those who were before them denied, so how was My censure?) ** فَكَيْفَ كانَ نَكِيرِ ** (so how was My censure?) refers to My censure (إِنْكَارِي) against them by sending down the punishment (الْعَذَاب). This is a consolation (تَسْلِيَة) for the Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and a threat (تَهْدِيد) to his people.

** أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا إِلَى الطَّيْرِ فَوْقَهُمْ صافَّاتٍ وَيَقْبِضْنَ مَا يُمْسِكُهُنَّ إِلَّا الرَّحْمَنُ إِنَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ بَصِيرٌ ** (Have they not regarded the birds above them, spreading their wings and folding them? None sustains them except the All-beneficent. Indeed, He is watchful over all things.) ** صافَّاتٍ ** (spreading their wings) means spreading their wings (أَجْنِحَتَهُنَّ) in the air (الْجَوّ) when they fly, for when they spread them, they align their primary feathers (قَوَادِمَهَا). ** وَيَقْبِضْنَ ** (and folding them) means they fold them (يَضْمُمْنَهَا) when they strike their sides (جُنُوبَهُنَّ) with them, time after time, seeking help (الِاسْتِعَانَة) through this action for movement (التَّحَرُّك). ** ما يُمْسِكُهُنَ ** (None sustains them) in the air (الْجَوّ), contrary to nature (عَلَى خِلَافِ الطَّبْع), ** إِلَّا الرَّحْمنُ ** (except the All-beneficent), Whose mercy (رَحْمَتُهُ) encompasses all things (كُلّ شَيْء). ** إِنَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ بَصِيرٌ ** (Indeed, He is watchful over all things) meaning He knows how He ought to create it (يَنْبَغِي أَنْ يَخْلُقَهُ).

** أَمَّنْ هذَا الَّذِي هُوَ جُنْدٌ لَكُمْ يَنْصُرُكُمْ مِنْ دُونِ الرَّحْمنِ ** (Is there anyone who is a host for you to help you, other than the All-beneficent?) Meaning, have you not looked at the likes of these works (الصنايع) so that you may know Our power to punish you by way of sinking (خسف) or sending a violent wind (إرسال حاصب)? Or is this one whom you worship besides Allah a host for you who helps you besides Allah, or [prevents Him from] sending His punishment upon you? So it is like His saying: أَمْ لَهُمْ آلِهَةٌ ** (Or do they have gods?) who prevent them besides Us. And in it is an indication that they believed in the second category (القسم الثاني). إِنِ الْكافِرُونَ إِلَّا فِي غُرُورٍ ** (The faithless are only in delusion.) They have no reliance (معتمد) for them.

**(٢١) أَمَّنْ هذَا الَّذِي يَرْزُقُكُمْ إِنْ أَمْسَكَ رِزْقَهُ ** (21) Or who is it that will provide for you if He withholds His provision?

(This refers to) His withholding of the rain and the rest of the means (الأسباب) that procure and deliver it to you. بَلْ لَجُّوا ** They persisted (or: they went to extremes) فِي عُتُوٍّ ** in obstinacy (عناد), aversion (نُفُورٍ), and fleeing (شِراد) from the truth, from which their natures recoil.

** أَفَمَنْ يَمْشِي مُكِبًّا عَلَى وَجْهِهِ ** (Is he who walks prone on his face) stumbles every hour and falls upon his face due to the ruggedness of his path, such that it is not worthy of being traversed, ** أَهْدَى أَمَّنْ يَمْشِي سَوِيًّا عَلَى صِرَاطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ ** (better guided, or he who walks upright on a straight path?) standing upright, safe from stumbling, عَلَى صِرَاطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ (on a straight path) whose parts and direction are level, and which is suitable for traversing. The intent is a similitude for the polytheist (المشرك) and the monotheist (الموحّد) by the travelers (السّالكين), and for the two religions (الدّينين) by the two paths (المسلكين).

In al-Kafi and al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Hearts are four: a heart in which there is hypocrisy (نفاق) and faith (ايمان), a heart turned upside down (منكوس), a sealed heart (مطبوع), and a bright, luminous heart (أزهر أنور). He said: As for the sealed heart (المطبوع), it is the heart of the hypocrite (المنافق). As for the bright heart (الأزهر), it is the heart of the believer (المؤمن); if Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, grants Him something, he is grateful, and if He tests him, he is patient. As for the heart turned upside down (المنكوس), it is the heart of the polytheist (المشرك). Then He recited this verse and mentioned the fourth.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: "Indeed, Allah set the example of one who deviates from the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him) as one who walks prone on his face, not guided to his affair. And He made the one who follows Him upright (سويّاً) on a straight path (صراطٍ مستقيم). And the straight path (الصراط المستقيم) is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him)."

**(٢٣) قُلْ هُوَ الَّذِي أَنْشَأَكُمْ وَجَعَلَ لَكُمُ السَّمْعَ وَالْأَبْصارَ وَالْأَفْئِدَةَ ** (23) Say, 'It is He who produced you, and made for you hearing, eyesight, and hearts.' So that you may hear His admonitions, look at His works, and reflect and take heed. ** قَلِيلاً ما تَشْكُرُونَ ** 'Little do you thank!' By using them in that for which they were created.

(٢٤) ** قُلْ هُوَ الَّذِي ذَرَأَكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَإِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ ** (24) Say, ‘It is He who created you in the earth, and toward Him you will be mustered.’ for the recompense (للجزاء). (٢٥) ** وَيَقُولُونَ مَتى هذَا الْوَعْدُ ** (25) And they say, ‘When will this promise be, meaning the gathering (الحشر) ** إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** if you are truthful?’ They mean the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the believers. (٢٦) ** قُلْ إِنَّمَا الْعِلْمُ ** (26) Say, ‘The knowledge [of it] is only meaning the knowledge of its time (علم وقته) ** عِنْدَ اللهِ ** with Allah, no one besides Him is informed of it ** وَإِنَّما أَنَا نَذِيرٌ مُبِينٌ. ** and I am only a manifest warner.’ (٢٧) ** فَلَمَّا رَأَوْهُ زُلْفَةً ** (27) But when they see it near, meaning possessing nearness (ذا قرب) (1). ** سِيئَتْ وُجُوهُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** the faces of the faithless will be distressed, Sadness (الكآبة) will appear upon them, and its sight will grieve them. ** وَقِيلَ هذَا الَّذِي كُنْتُمْ بِهِ تَدَّعُونَ ** and it will be said, ‘This is what you used to call for!’ meaning: you seek and hasten through supplication (الدعاء).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): This was revealed concerning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and his companions who did what they did (i.e., their deeds). They will see the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in the most enviable of places for them, and their faces will be distressed, and it will be said: ‘This is what you used to call for!’ (هذَا الَّذِي كُنْتُمْ بِهِ تَدَّعُونَ)—that is, the one whose name you falsely claimed (انتحلتم به اسمه). And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): When they see the status (مكان) of Ali with the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, the faces of the faithless will be distressed (سِيئَتْ وُجُوهُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا), meaning those who denied his virtue (فضله). And narrated from al-A'mash, who said: When they see the nearness (الزلفى) that Ali ibn Abi Talib has with Allah, the faces of the faithless will be distressed (سِيئَتْ وُجُوهُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا). Al-Qummi narrated that he said: When the Day of Resurrection occurs, and the enemies of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) look at him and at the honor (الكرامة) and the noble, great status (المنزلة الشريفة العظيمة) that Allah has bestowed upon him, and the Banner of Praise (لواء الحمد) is in his hand, and He is at the Pond (الحوض), giving drink and preventing others, the faces of his enemies will turn black, and it will be said to them: ‘This is what you used to call for!’ (هذَا الَّذِي كُنْتُمْ بِهِ تَدَّعُونَ)—meaning his status (منزلته), his position (موضعه), and his name (اسمه).

footnote (١) Meaning the Day of Badr (*يوم بدر*). And it is said: upon witnessing it (*معاينة*). And it is said: the wording is past tense (*ماضٍ*) but the intended meaning is the future (*المستقبل*).

(٢٨) The Almighty's saying: ** قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِنْ أَهْلَكَنِيَ اللهُ ** He causes me to die, وَمَنْ مَعِيَ ** meaning, from the believers, أَوْ رَحِمَنا ** by delaying our appointed times (آجالنا), فَمَنْ يُجِيرُ الْكافِرِينَ مِنْ عَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ ** meaning, no one will save them from the punishment, whether we die or we remain. And this is a response to their saying: نَتَرَبَّصُ بِهِ رَيْبَ الْمَنُونِ.**

(28) Say, 'Tell me, if Allah destroys me and those who are with me, or has mercy on us, who will protect the faithless from a painful punishment?'

(٢٩) ** قُلْ هُوَ الرَّحْمنُ (29) Say, 'He is the All-beneficent, Whom I call you to, the Master of all blessings. آمَنَّا بِهِ وَعَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْنا فَسَتَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ هُوَ فِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ We have faith in Him, and in Him we put our trust. Soon you will know who is in manifest error.' From among us and among you. And it was recited with the yāʾ (ياء).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): فَسَتَعْلَمُونَ (Soon you will know) O company of those who deny, when the message of my Lord informed you concerning the wilāyah (ولاية) of Ali and the Imams (peace be upon them) after him, مَنْ هُوَ فِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ (who is in manifest error). Thus was it revealed.

(٣٠) قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِنْ أَصْبَحَ ماؤُكُمْ غَوْراً ** (30) Say, 'Tell me, if your water were to sink [into the ground], [meaning] sinking (غائراً) into the earth such that buckets cannot reach it, فَمَنْ يَأْتِيكُمْ بِماءٍ مَعِينٍ (then who will bring you running water?) [meaning] flowing (جار) or visible (ظاهر), easy to obtain (سهل التناول).

Al-Qummi said: أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِنْ أَصْبَحَ (Tell me, if) your Imam becomes absent (غائباً), **فَمَنْ يَأْتِيكُمْ ** (then who will bring you) an Imam like him?

And narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: ماؤُكُمْ ** (your water) refers to your gates (أبوابكم), the Imams (peace be upon them), and the Imams are the gates (أبواب) of Allah. فَمَنْ يَأْتِيكُمْ بِماءٍ مَعِينٍ (then who will bring you running water?) means: who will bring you the knowledge (علم) of the Imam?

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): If your Imam is absent from you, **فَمَنْ يَأْتِيكُمْ ** (then who will bring you) a new Imam?

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about its interpretation (تأويلها), and He said: If you lose your Imam and do not see him, what will you do?

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: This [Ayah] was revealed concerning the Imam al-Qa'im (peace be upon him). He says: إِنْ أَصْبَحَ ** (if) your Imam becomes absent (غائباً) from you, and you do not know where He is, فَمَنْ يَأْتِيكُمْ (then who will bring you) a manifest Imam (إمام ظاهر) who brings you the news (أخبار) of the heavens and the earth, and the lawful (حلال) and the unlawful (حرام) of Allah? Then He said: By Allah, the interpretation (تأويل) of this Ayah has not yet come, but its interpretation must surely come.

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites تَبَارَكَ الَّذِي بِيَدِهِ الْمُلْكُ (Blessed is He in Whose Hand is the dominion) in the prescribed prayer (المكتوبة) before sleeping, he will remain in the security (أمان) of Allah until morning, and in His security on the Day of Resurrection until he enters Paradise. O Allah, grant us the recitation of it.

SURAH AL-QALAM

(It is also called Surah Nun (سُورة ن). It is Makkan. Ibn Abbas said: From its beginning up to His saying سَنَسِمُهُ عَلَى الْخُرْطُومِ ** (We shall brand him on the snout) is Makkan, and what follows it up to His saying لَوْ كانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ ** (if they knew) is Madani, and what follows that up to His saying يَكْتُبُونَ (they write) is Makkan, and what follows that is Madani. It consists of fifty-two Ayahs by consensus (بالإجماع).)

بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)
